description:
Implement Image Loading Placeholders and Fallback UI

Add placeholder visuals during image loading and graceful fallback displays for missing or failed-to-load images throughout the application.

Requirements:
- Display skeleton/blur placeholder while images are loading to prevent layout shift
- Show a styled fallback UI (icon, text, or placeholder graphic) when images fail to load or are missing
- Ensure fallback is visually consistent with the application's design system
- Handle different image aspect ratios and sizes appropriately
- Consider accessibility by adding appropriate alt text for fallback states
- Apply to all image components across the application (avatars, thumbnails, hero images, etc.)
- Optimize placeholder size to match final image dimensions
- Provide smooth transition from placeholder to loaded image

Edge cases to consider:
- Slow network connections with extended loading times
- 404 errors for missing image URLs
- Broken or corrupted image files
- Images with empty or null src attributes
- Network failures or timeouts during image fetch
